domains.rationale: Major UK/AU/US international news publisher and primary Guardian Content API source used for health story discovery.
Australian Broadcasting Corporation — the national public broadcaster and most trusted source for Australian health and science journalism.
Global wire service providing authoritative, lightly editorialised health and medical news with high factual accuracy standards.
Associated Press wire service — not-for-profit global newswire recognised for rigorous fact-checking and medical reporting accuracy.
Sydney Morning Herald — major Australian metropolitan daily serving the NSW market with dedicated health and science coverage.
The Age — major Australian metropolitan daily serving the Victorian market with consistent health, medicine, and public-policy reporting.
News Corp Australia's national digital masthead — broad reach across Australian general public and frequent health-news publisher.
Nine Network's national news platform — high Australian audience reach with dedicated health and consumer-medicine segments.
Special Broadcasting Service — Australian public multicultural broadcaster with strong coverage of health equity and minority health topics.
Australian Financial Review — authoritative source for health-industry business news, private-hospital policy, and pharmaceutical sector reporting.
Australian Institute of Health and Welfare — official federal statistical agency publishing primary health and welfare data underpinning all national health reports.
Australian Bureau of Statistics — the national statistical authority whose census, mortality, and population-health datasets are primary sources for demographic health claims.
Australian Department of Health and Aged Care — primary federal health-policy, program, and regulatory guidance source.
Royal Australian College of General Practitioners — peak GP professional body whose clinical guidelines and position statements govern primary-care practice in Australia.
Australian Medical Association — national doctor advocacy body whose policy positions and media statements represent the broader medical profession.
Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency — the national regulator responsible for registration and accreditation of all health professions.
Medical Board of Australia (under AHPRA) — the specific regulatory board setting standards and managing complaints for registered medical practitioners.
Royal College of Nursing Aotearoa New Zealand — peak nursing professional body providing clinical guidelines and advocacy relevant to trans-Tasman health coverage.
New Zealand Ministry of Health — primary NZ government source for public-health policy, statistics, and national health-strategy documents.
Royal New Zealand College of General Practitioners — peak NZ GP body whose clinical guidelines and workforce data inform trans-Tasman primary-care reporting.
World Health Organization — the UN's directing and coordinating authority on international public health, publishing globally authoritative guidelines and surveillance data.
NCBI/PubMed at the US National Library of Medicine — the world's largest index of peer-reviewed biomedical and life-sciences literature.
Nature portfolio — one of the world's most cited multidisciplinary science publishers including Nature Medicine and Nature Human Behaviour.
The Lancet — one of the world's oldest and most influential peer-reviewed general medical journals, publishing landmark clinical research and health-policy analysis.
Medical Journal of Australia — the premier peer-reviewed Australian clinical journal, publishing original research and guidelines directly relevant to Australian medical practice.
The BMJ (British Medical Journal) — high-impact peer-reviewed general medical journal widely read by Australian clinicians and regularly cited in health-policy debates.
